title: Finite Chain-Complex Torsion Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite chain-complex torsion QF_LIA/Diophantine regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: The fixed chain complex d1(e)=2v makes H0 = Z/2, so a malformed row claiming v is already a boundary isolates the impossible integer equation 2*k = 1.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional torsion or universal-coefficient rows only when they add distinct Smith-normal-form, quotient, finite-field, or proof-reconstruction pressure; keep general homology invariance and universal coefficient theorems in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: State the finite free abelian chain groups, ordered bases, integer boundary matrices, and boundary-composition check explicitly.
Replay the Smith-normal-form diagonal and torsion factors with exact integer arithmetic rather than trusting a solver verdict.
Keep the promoted bad torsion-generator row tied to a committed SMT-LIB artifact and checked QF_LIA/Diophantine evidence.
Add larger Smith-normal-form and quotient-module proof resources only after they have deterministic replay data and route regressions.
Keep universal coefficient theorems, Ext/Tor functor laws, chain homotopy invariance, and topological invariance under Lean horizon until kernel-checked proof routes exist.
